tossups.question: The Fifth Monarchist sect believed this event would allow for the rule of the saints that would prepare for the Second Coming. A speech given at this event was written down only by the priest William Juxon, who had spent the previous day praying with the speech giver. This event spurred Alexis I (“the first”) to restrict Muscovy Company agents to trading in Arkh·angelsk (“ar-KONG-gilsk”). The people responsible for this event were not granted amnesty by the (*) Indemnity and Oblivion Act. This event took place at the Banqueting House in Whitehall, after Pride’s Purge got rid of those who favored negotiation from Parliament. This event occurred after a war in which its victim allied with Scottish Covenanters against the New Model Army. For 10 points, name this 1649 event in which the Stuart Monarchy was temporarily ended by Oliver Cromwell.
tossups.answer: the execution of Charles I [accept beheading or synonyms for execution; prompt on execution of Charles; prompt on the trial of Charles I until “Banqueting House”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: the execution of Charles I [accept beheading or synonyms for execution; prompt on execution of Charles; prompt on the trial of Charles I until "Banqueting House"]
tossups.question_sanitized: The Fifth Monarchist sect believed this event would allow for the rule of the saints that would prepare for the Second Coming. A speech given at this event was written down only by the priest William Juxon, who had spent the previous day praying with the speech giver. This event spurred Alexis I ("the first") to restrict Muscovy Company agents to trading in Arkhangelsk ("ar-KONG-gilsk"). The people responsible for this event were not granted amnesty by the (*) Indemnity and Oblivion Act. This event took place at the Banqueting House in Whitehall, after Pride's Purge got rid of those who favored negotiation from Parliament. This event occurred after a war in which its victim allied with Scottish Covenanters against the New Model Army. For 10 points, name this 1649 event in which the Stuart Monarchy was temporarily ended by Oliver Cromwell.

bonuses.leadin: This building features Ionic and Composite order pilasters and alternating segmental and triangular pediments over its first-floor windows. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building designed by Inigo (“IN-uh-go”) Jones that introduced the style of Palladio to England.
This later London architect used a similar architectural vocabulary including double Corinthian columns for his redesign of St. Paul’s Cathedral after the Great Fire of London in 1666.
Wren blended Baroque elements like doubled pilasters and a Corinthian colonnade with Gothic forms in the steeple for this other London building, which he also redesigned after the Great Fire.
answers: Banqueting House at Whitehall Palace [prompt on Whitehall Palace]
answers_sanitized: Banqueting House at Whitehall Palace [prompt on Whitehall Palace]

bonuses.leadin: In one painting that is part of this series, two cherubs holding roses float above a scene in which Minerva accompanies the personification of two nations in placing the title object on the head of the man at the center of that painting. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this series of paintings that includes The Union of the Crowns, as well as an apotheosis, and ovals depicting things like the triumph of the virtues over the vices.
The ceiling of the Banqueting House at Whitehall was painted with the various achievements of James I by this Baroque artist.
Rubens may have been inspired to paint the ceiling of the Banqueting House by a ceiling painting in the Doge’s palace titled The Triumph of Venice by this Italian.
answers: the ceiling of the Banqueting House at Whitehall [accept Rubens’ paintings of James I but prompt if they just say “James I” without giving the artist; do not read alternate answers]
answers_sanitized: the ceiling of the Banqueting House at Whitehall [accept Rubens' paintings of James I but prompt if they just say "James I" without giving the artist; do not read alternate answers]

parts_sanitized: Identify this series of paintings that includes The Union of the Crowns, as well as an apotheosis, and ovals depicting things like the triumph of the virtues over the vices.
The ceiling of the Banqueting House at Whitehall was painted with the various achievements of James I by this Baroque artist.
Rubens may have been inspired to paint the ceiling of the Banqueting House by a ceiling painting in the Doge's palace titled The Triumph of Venice by this Italian.

bonuses.leadin: Three men on horseback hold spears pointing towards the central action of this painting, which includes a shirtless, scared man thrusting a sword. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting in which the two main animals are positioned on top of each other, one in black, and the other in shades of olive and grey, both with large teeth pointed towards the viewer.
The Hippopotamus and Crocodile Hunt was painted by this artist of The Massacre of the Innocents and several paintings of Marie de Medici who is known for his fleshy nudes.
Rubens spent time in England to paint a nine-paneled Apotheosis of James I for the ceiling of this building found in the Palace of Whitehall. It was designed by Inigo Jones.

Rubens spent time in England to paint a nine-paneled Apotheosis of James I for the ceiling of this building found in the Palace of Whitehall. It was designed by Inigo Jones.

bonuses.leadin: This building stopped being used for masques when it was discovered that smoke was damaging The Apotheosis of James I, a ceiling mural painted by Rubens. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this British royal building at Whitehall erected in 1622 whose facade places four sets of Ionic and Corinthian columns within two sets of pilasters on the outer bays.

The Banqueting House at Whitehall is one of the few surviving buildings designed by this architect, who was heavily influenced by Andrea Palladio. His rectangular design for Covent Garden was inspired by the Place Royale in Paris.

This other British architect worked with men like Robert Hooke to construct several buildings in the aftermath of the Great Fire of London. He's best known for constructing the new St. Paul's Cathedral.
answers: The Banqueting House at Whitehall [prompt on "Whitehall Palace"]
answers_sanitized: The Banqueting House at Whitehall [prompt on "Whitehall Palace"]
